objective.subject: Mathematics
objective.statement: Identify, generate and explain equivalent fractions using visual and numerical representations.
misconceptions: Thinks a larger denominator always means a larger fraction.
Changes the numerator or denominator without changing both by the same factor.
Matches fractions by visual area without checking the whole is the same size.
objective.parent_explanation: Can recognise and create equivalent fractions, and explain why two written fractions can have the same value.
objective.teacher_evidence: Uses area models, number lines and scaling to justify fraction equivalence.

activities.title: I can show when two fractions are equal in value.
activities.prompt: Split one half into smaller equal parts without changing its value.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which two bars show the same whole?
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Equivalent fractions need the same whole.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Aligned and misaligned fraction bars compare whole sizes.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Split one half into smaller equal parts without changing its value.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce equivalence as repartitioning the same amount.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The shaded amount stays the same, even when the pieces get smaller.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A half bar subdivides into two quarters and then four eighths.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: One half is equivalent to two quarters.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Connect area model to symbolic numerator and denominator scaling.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Both the shaded parts and total parts doubled, so the value stayed the same.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Use the scale lens to build a fraction equivalent to 3/5.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise multiplying numerator and denominator by the same factor.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Whatever factor you use, use it on the top and the bottom.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A symbolic fraction links to a fraction bar that repartitions.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Do 2/3 and 4/6 land on the same point?
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Equivalent fractions land at the same place on a number line.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two stacked number lines align 2/3 and 4/6.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Ari why 3/6 is not bigger than 1/2.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Six pieces sounds like more. Can you show me why the value is the same?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion compares a half bar with a sixths bar.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Equal in value, even if written differently.
vocabulary.audio_script: Equivalent fractions have the same value.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two fraction bars of the same length with different partitions.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A number used to multiply or divide another number.
vocabulary.audio_script: A factor can scale the numerator and denominator together.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A x2 lens doubling both parts of a fraction.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The full amount the fraction is part of.
vocabulary.audio_script: To compare fractions fairly, the wholes must be the same size.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two equal whole bars aligned side by side.

manipulative.purpose: Connect equivalent fractions across area, number-line and symbolic forms.
manipulative.interaction_notes: Learners subdivide bars, apply scale lenses, align number lines and build equivalent symbols.
manipulative.accessibility_notes: Patterns and labels supplement colour; reduced-motion uses static synced panels.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use clean lab colours and avoid high-speed subdivision animations.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use step-by-step static fraction panels and line alignment.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Narrate fraction names, equivalent meaning and scale steps.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Pair symbolic fractions with visual bars and spoken explanations.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Keep one representation active at a time before syncing all three.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow learners to slow subdivision and inspect numerator-denominator changes.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Use 'test the value' language for wrong symbolic matches.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach equivalent, numerator, denominator, whole, factor and same value.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Prevent false equivalence caused by comparing different-sized wholes.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: paired bars + same/different whole prompt + alignment tool
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Visuals must make whole-size differences deliberate, not accidental.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Generate equivalent fractions by subdividing area models.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: base fraction + subdivision factor + shaded bar
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Start with halves, thirds and fifths before mixed denominators.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Apply the same multiplier or divisor to numerator and denominator.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: fraction + factor + missing numerator or denominator
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Distractors should change only one part for misconception detection.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Confirm equivalent fractions land on the same position.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: stacked number lines + target fractions + same point task
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use clear tick labels and avoid visual clutter.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Explain equivalence and transfer to tenths, hundredths and simple measure contexts.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: context model + equivalent symbol + because sentence stem
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Teacher review needed for concise explanations and acceptable alternatives.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks a larger denominator always means a larger fraction.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner selects 1/6 as larger than 1/2 because 6 is larger than 2.
repair_sequence: Show both fractions on equal whole bars.
Highlight that sixths are smaller pieces.
Align equivalent positions on a number line.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner compares fractions by value rather than denominator size.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Changes the numerator or denominator without changing both by the same factor.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner converts 3/5 to 6/5 or 3/10 when scaling by 2.
repair_sequence: Place a linked scale lens over numerator and denominator.
Apply the factor to both numbers.
Check the visual bar still shows the same value.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner generates an equivalent fraction by scaling both parts.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Matches fractions by visual area without checking the whole is the same size.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner accepts equal-looking shaded regions from different-sized wholes.
repair_sequence: Resize one whole to match the other.
Ask learner to compare again using equal wholes.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner rejects unfair comparisons with different whole sizes.

feedback.companion_prompt: Teach Ari why 3/6 is not bigger than 1/2.
feedback.parent_summary: Can recognise and create equivalent fractions, and explain why two written fractions can have the same value.
feedback.teacher_evidence: Uses area models, number lines and scaling to justify fraction equivalence.
next_actions: Interleave equivalent fractions with fraction comparison.
Connect tenths and hundredths once equivalent value is secure.
Use teach-back explanations in spaced review.

hints: Look for the same shaded amount.
One half can be split into two quarters.
questions.explanation: 1/2 and 2/4 are equivalent because they shade the same amount of the same whole.

body.prompt: Multiply 3/5 by a scale factor of 2. What equivalent fraction do you get?

hints: Multiply the denominator by 2 as well.
questions.explanation: 3/5 is equivalent to 6/10 because both numerator and denominator are multiplied by 2.

body.prompt: Do 2/3 and 4/6 land on the same point on the number line?

hints: Sixths are smaller pieces, but there are twice as many shaded pieces.
questions.explanation: 2/3 and 4/6 are equivalent, so they land at the same point.

reward_payload.explanation: Can recognise and create equivalent fractions, and explain why two written fractions can have the same value.
reward_payload.companion_prompt: Teach Ari why 3/6 is not bigger than 1/2.
